description:
You are an incident responder who has been asked to capture a
  single, reliable DNS resolution result for the investigation record.

  1. Query the A-record for the hostname “localhost” from the command
     line.  (Any standards-compliant utility such as dig, drill, or
     getent is acceptable as long as it produces the correct answer.)

  2. Create a new plain-text file at the exact path
     /home/user/incidents/resolution.log
     (make the directory first if it does not already exist).

  3. Write the result to that file using the **three-line, KEY=VALUE
     format shown below – nothing more, nothing less, no blank lines,
     no extra spaces**:

     HOSTNAME=localhost
     IPV4=127.0.0.1
     RCODE=NOERROR

     • HOSTNAME must literally be “localhost”.
     • IPV4 must be exactly “127.0.0.1”.
     • RCODE must be the textual DNS status returned by your lookup
       (“NOERROR” when the query succeeds).

  4. Verify the file is saved with those three lines exactly.  The
     automated test will read /home/user/incidents/resolution.log and
     must find an identical, line-by-line match.

  No additional output or artefacts are required.
truth:
The grading script will assert that the file
  /home/user/incidents/resolution.log exists and that its contents are
  byte-for-byte:

  HOSTNAME=localhost
  IPV4=127.0.0.1
  RCODE=NOERROR
name: task_000000_458d5f85
